expected_effect: Retain selective anti-arb protection without paying the full symmetric fee cost of the dead split-side family.
expected_failure_signature: If the shell stays engaged too long, average fee rises without enough incremental edge; if it refills too slowly, the line misses true stress.
expected_win_condition: Improve anti-arb and weak slices while staying below the fee drag that blocked promotion in the strongest raw branch.
hidden_coupling_removed: Persistent protection and one-sided stress response should not spend from the same untracked risk budget.
rationale: Split risk spend into a small always-on floor, a stress shell keyed to recent arb-loss consumption, and a one-sided extension shell that refills slowly.
title: Drawdown-budgeted risk shell
why_not_coefficient_retune: This introduces explicit budget accounting and refill semantics rather than another regime toggle or additive surcharge retune.
